changes.description: 'Check for Updates' now shows a Windows toast notification with the result instead of silently logging — tells you whether an update is available or you're up to date
Auto-update now triggers for all available updates on startup, not just mandatory ones — the agent will download and apply v1.2.1 automatically
Windows toast notification on startup when a new version is detected, before auto-update begins downloading
Session timing — tracks how long each card takes from scan to slab completion, with per-operator productivity stats (cards/hour, averages, fastest/slowest)
Scanner quality monitoring — analyzes brightness, contrast, sharpness, and noise for every scan. Warns when quality degrades from baseline calibration
Scanner calibration checks — scan a reference card to establish baseline quality metrics. Future scans compared against this baseline
Image tamper detection — every scanned image is immediately SHA-256 hashed and HMAC-signed with the station identity. Cryptographic proof of origin
Chain of custody logging — records exactly which station, operator, scanner, and time produced each scan. Full audit trail per card serial
Print job tracking with ink usage estimates — tracks total prints, estimates remaining ink, and predicts when cartridge replacement is needed
Offline card cache — caches failed cloud API calls locally and syncs when connectivity resumes. Manual sync trigger available
Cloud analytics endpoints — population reports, grade distribution, defect heatmaps, and operator bias detection
Local telemetry SQLite database (data/agent_telemetry.db) stores all metrics separately from main app DB
Agent /status endpoint now includes telemetry summary (productivity stats, pending sync count)
Custom rocket icon in the Windows system tray — blue circle with white rocket, visible at all icon sizes
Rich tray menu showing: version, station ID, server status, live hardware summary (scanner/printer/NFC), and quick links
Auto-start on Windows boot — agent registers itself in the Windows startup registry on first run
'Start with Windows' toggle in tray menu — easily enable/disable auto-start without editing the registry manually
'Open Grading Station' menu item — launches rktgradingstation.co.uk in your default browser with one click
'Check for Updates' menu item — manually trigger an update check from the tray
Double-clicking the tray icon opens the grading station website directly
Agent exe now has the rocket icon embedded (visible in taskbar, file explorer, and Alt+Tab)
Fixed 'Failed to load Python DLL' error — switched from --onedir to --onefile build so the exe is fully self-contained with no external dependencies
Fixed crash on startup caused by PyInstaller's --noconsole mode setting sys.stderr to None, which broke uvicorn's logging initialisation
Fixed auto-update download failing to follow S3 presigned URL redirects — added follow_redirects=True to httpx stream
Reduced agent exe size from 84MB to 37MB by creating a clean build environment without ML libraries (torch, paddleocr, scipy)
Created dedicated agent_requirements.txt with only the dependencies the agent actually needs
S3 download now uses 'latest.exe' key so the download endpoint doesn't need updating on each version bump

title: Initial Release — Hardware Bridge for Cloud Grading Station

changes.description: Scanner integration via Windows Image Acquisition (WIA) — supports Canon LiDE 400 and other WIA-compatible flatbed scanners
Epson C6000 label printer support via Windows GDI — renders and prints slab insert labels at up to 1200 DPI with configurable dimensions
NFC tag programming via PC/SC (ACR1252U reader) — supports both NTag213 (simple static URL) and NTag424 DNA (SUN/SDM secure cryptographic URL)
Auto-update system — checks cloud server for new versions on startup, downloads and applies updates automatically for PyInstaller builds
Mock mode for all hardware — develop and test the full workflow without physical scanner, printer, or NFC reader connected
Runs as a lightweight FastAPI server on localhost:8742 — browser communicates via REST API and WebSocket
System tray mode (--tray flag) — runs silently in the Windows taskbar with status menu showing version, station ID, and hardware state
Hardware status endpoint (/agent/status) — reports connected devices, mock mode state, agent version, and station identity
Station connection indicator in the cloud UI navbar — shows 'Desktop Mode' locally or 'Station: [ID]' when connected via cloud
Download button and changelog link added to the sidebar footer for easy access to the latest agent installer
